output:
Verification executed for T10. The throwaway reproduction script for the timeline-store mounted/sync/reset path passed and was deleted. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed. The targeted timeline/provider suite passed, the broader `src/tools/video-editor` suite passed (`54` files / `344` tests), and the full `npm test` suite passed (`878` files / `4544` tests).

The milestone is still blocked at the gate. A temporary jsdom telemetry harness confirmed `TimelineCanvas` stayed within the scroll budget, but it also showed a row-local clamp update still caused both `SortableRow` action renders to run (`2 -> 4` calls instead of the expected `2 -> 3`). I tried one focused revise cycle in `TrackListRenderer.tsx` by memoizing `SortableContext` item ids, but the row-isolation result did not improve. I also could not complete the required 10-minute manual browser validation for drag, resize, rapid selection, full keyboard shortcut coverage, playback, AgentChat bridge behavior, or pending-add-from-URL from this terminal-only environment, so I did not accept the milestone.
deviations: Could not perform the required browser-only 10-minute manual validation from the terminal environment.

Stopped at a blocked gate instead of accepting the milestone because the temporary row-isolation telemetry harness still showed both `SortableRow` action renders running on a row-local clamp update after one focused revise cycle.

task_updates.executor_notes: Verification ran end to end on the current worktree. A throwaway reproduction script exercised the provider-scoped timeline store mounted/sync/reset path via `createTimelineStore`, printed `timeline-store-repro:ok`, and was deleted immediately afterward. Targeted timeline/provider coverage passed (`VideoEditorProvider`, `TimelineCanvas`, `PreviewPersistence`, `useClipDrag`, `useClipResize`, `useExternalDrop`, `useTimelinePersistence`, `usePollSync`, `AgentChat`), the broader `src/tools/video-editor` suite passed (54 files / 344 tests), and the full suite passed with `npm test` (878 files / 4544 tests). `npx tsc --noEmit` is clean after the one verification-driven code tweak. Telemetry evidence is mixed: a temporary jsdom harness confirmed `TimelineCanvas` stayed within `<= 2` renders for an isolated scroll event, but the same harness showed a row-local clamp update still triggered both `SortableRow` action renders (`renderAction` call count `2 -> 4` instead of the expected `2 -> 3`). I attempted one focused revise cycle by memoizing `SortableContext` item ids in `TrackListRenderer.tsx`, but the row-isolation result did not improve. Per the milestone gate, I am surfacing this instead of calling it green. I also cannot honestly claim the required 10-minute manual browser validation for drag, resize, rapid selection, full keyboard shortcut coverage, playback, AgentChat bridge behavior, or the pending-add-from-URL flow from this shell-only environment.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Partially. I ran the throwaway reproduction script, `npx tsc --noEmit`, the relevant existing timeline/provider tests, the broader `src/tools/video-editor` suite, the temporary telemetry harness, and the full `npm test` suite. The automated suites are green, but the row-isolation telemetry harness still shows both `SortableRow` instances rerender on a row-local clamp update, and the required manual 10-minute browser validation for drag/resize/selection/playback/AgentChat/pending-add was not possible from this terminal environment. I therefore did not accept the milestone.
